title: store name of stage inside metadata/labels
body:
### Discussed in https://github.com/containers/buildah/discussions/4728
Originally posted by **tobwen** April 9, 2023 ## summary When building images with **named** multiple stages, the name isn't stored anywhere. Making the name available in metadata (for example in labels) would make developing and debugging way easier. ## example ``` sh podman build -t stages_demo - <> /podman.txt # stage 2 FROM findme AS release RUN echo "world" >> /podman.txt EOF [1/2] STEP 1/2: FROM alpine AS findme [1/2] STEP 2/2: RUN echo "hello" >> /podman.txt --> c54f74d6cc9 [2/2] STEP 1/2: FROM c54f74d6cc97487a1116841a2b2d380a387a38d111bb7b28116ed4d3f2110251 AS release [2/2] STEP 2/2: RUN echo "world" >> /podman.txt [2/2] COMMIT stages_demo --> 9669170f524 Successfully tagged localhost/stages_demo:latest 9669170f5249c48ef090de9748c20781956d97304972f19818c91399a3be32ab ``` ## test Neither `findme`, nor `release` can be found. ``` sh # docker image inspect c54f74d6cc97487a1116841a2b2d380a387a38d111bb7b28116ed4d3f2110251 | grep -i findme; echo $? 1 # docker image inspect 9669170f5249c48ef090de9748c20781956d97304972f19818c91399a3be32ab | grep -i findme; echo $? 1 # docker image inspect c54f74d6cc97487a1116841a2b2d380a387a38d111bb7b28116ed4d3f2110251 | grep -i release; echo $? 1 # docker image inspect 9669170f5249c48ef090de9748c20781956d97304972f19818c91399a3be32ab | grep -i release; echo $? 1 ```

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

@tobwen Could you please let us know why do you need such feature, I am trying to understand the use-case behind this requirement ? Just wondering in case we have a easier workaround for your use-case using existing features.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Since we never heard back, closing, reopen if you have feed back and still want this feature.

I'm so sorry, I completely forgot about this topic.

I'm building via the multistage method with 6 stages in total. Every now and then I want to peek into a stage. Luckily, the stages aren't deleted by default, but it's hard to find them.

During build, each stage gets a image ID only, for example:

``` dockerfile
# image ID: --> d57b5a995f2f
FROM docker://postgres:15-bookworm AS deps ...
RUN ...

# image ID: --> a8064d84a4f7
FROM deps AS cgal
RUN ...

# image ID --> 8469cdf2daba
FROM cgal AS proj
RUN...
```
Therefore I can `podman run -it a8064d84a4f7 bash`, but I need to take care during the build process to figure out which stage gets which image ID.

So it would be optimal if `42af5392` gets a hint, which stage it was in the Dockerfile, e.g. `FROM deps AS cgal`.

Ah i see your use-case now. One thing which you can use is: it prints image ID while building and also if nothing is changed in image and you rebuild it will `reprint` image ID which will be used as cache without changing anything in the final image. Does that works for you ? 

I mean feature request is valid but it sounds like a very very specific use-case ( which has a workaround ) WDYT ? Should we wait to see if more users need this before implementing this as a feature :)

```console
[1/2] STEP 1/2: FROM alpine
[1/2] STEP 2/2: RUN touch hey
--> Using cache 9d6d75a602fe7f417b96278116936aba54561fa2d937402bf01d884b0a852178
--> 9d6d75a602fe
[2/2] STEP 1/2: FROM busybox
[2/2] STEP 2/2: COPY --from=0 hey .
--> Using cache 2b2b4edb25b5d21a8187d68f3fc87a9a94738c9074231e309c9a6394dd008e23
[2/2] COMMIT test
--> 2b2b4edb25b5
Successfully tagged localhost/test:latest
2b2b4edb25b5d21a8187d68f3fc87a9a94738c9074231e309c9a6394dd008e23
```

> Ah i see your use-case now. One thing which you can use is: it prints image ID while building and also if nothing is changed in image and you rebuild it will `reprint` image ID which will be used as cache without changing anything in the final image. Does that works for you ?

At the moment I just log the output of _buildah_ with it. But sure, a rebuild would be a work-around.

But wouldn't it be a totally simple, low-threshold and compatible solution to put the information anywhere in the metadata?

For example `$ podman inspect 8459cdfbd2ba`:
``` json
          "Labels": {
               "io.buildah.version": "1.31.2"
               "io.buildah.stage_name": "FROM kebab AS döner" <-- HERE
          },
          "Annotations": {
               "org.opencontainers.image.base.digest": "sha256:41c3f372c160576268fd269c0717871d7f9080468e312bdb66f9510ba3981b54",
               "org.opencontainers.image.base.name": "docker.io/library/postgres:15-bookworm",
               "org.opencontainers.image.revision": "8a631b939a0b4197cb6bef49b50b6c40c80ddf5b",
               "org.opencontainers.image.source": "https://github.com/docker-library/postgres.git#8a631b939a0b4197cb6bef49b50b6c40c80ddf5b:15/bookworm",
               "org.opencontainers.image.url": "https://hub.docker.com/_/postgres",
               "org.opencontainers.image.version": "15.4"
                <-- OR ANYWHERE HERE -->
          }
```
